title: Agent Onboarding Kit
competency: 01. Toolchain Setup - Project rules, hooks, guardrails, and CLI or MCP wiring
mission: Configure a coding agent so it can understand and work on a repository like a new engineering teammate.
domain: Internal support case router with limited documentation, mixed data-access patterns, hidden ownership rules, and missing onboarding instructions.
outcome: A fresh coding agent can inspect the repository, follow documented boundaries, run the correct checks, and produce a reviewable change without repeated human re-explanation.
seededDefects: The repository has no agent rules or architecture map.
The data and routing layers use different naming conventions.
Testing and review expectations are spread across package scripts and sparse notes.
Ownership rules are only implied by code and sample data.
verificationGates: Agent setup files exist in .agent with concrete repository guidance.
AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md explains safe commands, avoided commands, and PR expectations.
Architecture notes name the app, service, data, and ownership boundaries.
Testing notes tell a fresh agent which commands prove the work.
agentWorkflow: Inspect package scripts, src folders, services, data, and docs before writing rules.
Map architecture boundaries from code rather than inventing a generic template.
Document safe commands and risky commands with repository-specific reasons.
Create the onboarding layer without refactoring unrelated application behavior.
